steps.title: Find the costs bar
steps.content: The graph shows three factors (convenience, costs, established behaviors) with the percentage of the 55 participants who mentioned each. Locate the bar labeled "costs" and read it against the vertical percentage axis.

steps.title: Read the height
steps.content: The costs bar reaches the 50% gridline, indicating roughly half of the interviewed participants mentioned costs as a factor.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '10%': far too low — no bar in the graph sits near 10%. Misread of the axis. ✗
  • Choice B — '95%': this matches the tallest bar (a different factor), not the costs bar. Wrong bar. ✗
  • Choice C — '25%': this corresponds to a different, shorter bar; the costs bar is taller than this. ✗
  • Choice D — '50%': matches the height of the costs bar at the 50% gridline. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
